# WI-QUIC-CRT-0025 - QUIC CRT client resumption-ticket snapshot and early-data gate work item

## Summary

Own opaque resumption-ticket snapshot on the managed client/runtime path and keep early data gated closed until that state exists.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0127

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0025

## Planned Changes

- A runtime-owned opaque resumption-ticket snapshot now captures the first accepted client-side ticket update from the existing ingress seam.
- The runtime exposes an explicit early-data gate that remains closed before and after snapshot capture.
- The bridge-state ingress seam remains the transient source of ticket bytes, but the runtime keeps its own copy.
- Focused requirement-home tests cover positive capture, absence before ingress, duplicate handling, server-role rejection, closed early-data behavior, and the public no-ticket/no-early-data guard.
- PSK derivation, 0-RTT packet processing, replay handoff, anti-replay guarantees, transfer, retry, key update, and any public API widening remain out of scope.

## Out of Scope

- public API widening
- ticket replay into a later connection
- PSK derivation
- resumed handshake support
- 0-RTT packet send/receive support
- anti-replay behavior
- broad support for arbitrary post-handshake TLS messages
- fake replay or fake resumption success paths
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- key update
- broader session-management infrastructure
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to owned ticket state or explicit gate closure

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for runtime-owned snapshot capture, absence before ingress, duplicate retention, server-role rejection, explicit early-data closure before and after capture, and the public no-ticket/no-early-data guard; run the current CRT/API/INT guards and the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep; run repo-local SpecTrace validation and render checks after the canonical JSON updates.

## Completion Notes

This is a landed narrow runtime-owned snapshot slice. It records opaque ticket ownership and an explicit closed gate only; it does not claim PSK derivation, replay handoff, 0-RTT support, or any public ticket/early-data promise.
